# How Mind Lab Pro Improves Reaction Times And Athletic Performance

- **Source:** https://edlatimore.com/mind-lab-pro-improves-reactions-times
- **Date:** 2026-03-16T04:00:00.000Z
- **Captured:** read-only public web scrape 2026-06-20 18:43
- **Length:** 29290 chars

## Body (verbatim, study/swipe)

Affiliate disclosure

This post contains affiliate links. If you purchase through my links, I earn a small commission at no extra cost to you. Thanks for supporting the site!

When it comes to athletic supplements, companies make many promises. But—to their credit—many of them actually deliver.

Creatine helps you get stronger and get more out of your training. Pre-workouts—stim or non-stim—can amp you up in the gym and boost your energy. Protein powder helps you reach your macros and build muscle. And if you throw in ingredients like L-citrulline, arginine, or taurine, you’ll even get a nice pump while you’re training.

Despite the supplement industry not being tightly regulated by the Food and Drug Administration (FDA), you can still find effective products if you do a bit of homework and stick with reputable brands.

Just because something isn’t FDA-regulated doesn’t mean it’s ineffective. Many supplements have a long body of research supporting their benefits—the FDA simply doesn’t regulate them the way it does pharmaceutical drugs.

For athletes competing under the rules of the World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA), there’s another layer to consider: anything you take has to be legal in competition. I sometimes joke with people in the training world that if you want to know whether something really works, check whether it’s banned by WADA.

All of this highlights an interesting gap in the supplement world, and one that I dealt with during my boxing career.

There are plenty of substances—both legal and banned—that improve physical performance. But when it comes to improving the mental side of athletic performance, the options are surprisingly limited.

And that’s strange, because your personal best is won in the mind before it ever shows up in your body.

Split-second decisions. Clean technique under fatigue. Staying composed when your heart rate spikes and your lungs are burning. These are mental skills just as much as physical ones.

I’ve always said that your mind will quit long before your body does—and research backs this up.

In one well-known endurance study, athletes who completed 90 minutes of mentally demanding work before cycling reached exhaustion much faster than those who didn’t. Their time to exhaustion dropped by about 15% simply because their brains were fatigued (Marcora et al., 2009).

Mental fatigue can directly limit physical performance.

This is where nootropics come in.

For athletes, the goal isn’t sketchy stimulants or banned performance enhancers. The goal is supporting the cognitive systems that drive performance in the first place—focus, reaction time, motivation, and the ability to stay calm under pressure.

Recently, I came across [Mind Lab Pro](https://www.mindlabpro.com/?a_aid=698b400c4a47f&a_bid=6d45f5c3), a nootropic supplement that combines several ingredients shown to support faster reaction time, clearer thinking, and mental endurance during training—all while remaining safe and compliant with WADA rules.

## **What Is Mind Lab Pro?**

Mind Lab Pro® is what the nootropics industry calls a “universal nootropic.” Instead of targeting just one cognitive function—like memory or focus—it’s designed to support multiple brain systems simultaneously.

For example, caffeine-based formulas focus almost entirely on stimulation and alertness. Memory-focused supplements often rely heavily on compounds like bacopa monnieri or ginkgo biloba to support recall and learning. Others, such as L-theanine products, are primarily used for relaxation and stress reduction.

Each of these can be useful, but they only address one piece of the cognitive puzzle. Athletic performance, on the other hand, depends on several mental systems working together—focus, reaction time, mental energy, motivation, and composure under pressure.

Mind Lab Pro aims to support all of those at once.

The supplement uses a stack of 11 research-backed nootropic ingredients, including compounds like citicoline, lion’s mane mushroom, bacopa monnieri, phosphatidylserine, L-theanine, N-acetyl L-tyrosine, rhodiola rosea, and maritime pine bark extract.

These ingredients work on different neurological pathways—supporting neurotransmitters, brain energy metabolism, neuroplasticity, and stress resilience.

In other words, instead of acting like a stimulant that simply forces the brain into overdrive, Mind Lab Pro is designed to optimize the biological systems that allow the brain to perform at its best in the first place.

Another important detail—especially for athletes—is that Mind Lab Pro is stimulant-free. There’s no caffeine or harsh stimulants in the formula. Instead, it relies on compounds that support sustained cognitive performance without the crash or jittery feeling associated with typical energy supplements.

This is particularly useful for sports that require calm focus and precision rather than pure stimulation.

From a research perspective, Mind Lab Pro is also unusual among nootropic supplements because the finished formula—not just the individual ingredients—has been tested in human clinical studies.

Multiple double-blind, placebo-controlled trials have examined its cognitive effects. In one study, participants taking Mind Lab Pro showed significant improvements in reaction time, information processing speed, and anticipation during cognitive testing.

Another randomized controlled study found that four weeks of supplementation significantly improved several types of memory, including auditory memory, visual memory, working memory, and delayed recall, compared to the placebo.

Across these studies, researchers have reported improvements such as:

- Faster information processing
- Better reaction time and anticipation
- Improved short- and long-term memory
- Stronger communication between brain regions measured by EEG

Taken together, these results suggest that Mind Lab Pro may support the types of cognitive abilities that directly influence athletic performance—fast thinking, mental endurance, and the ability to stay sharp under stress.

For athletes looking for a legal, research-backed cognitive performance supplement, that combination makes Mind Lab Pro stand out from most products in the nootropics category.

Before looking at how Mind Lab Pro has done in the laboratory settings and covering my own experiences, I want to dive into exactly how a nootropic improves other parameters of your performance.

#### [***Grab a bottle of Mind Lab Pro here***](https://www.mindlabpro.com/?a_aid=698b400c4a47f&a_bid=6d45f5c3)

## **Nootropics and the Mind–Body Connection Necessary for Athletic Performance**

Can nootropics improve athletic performance—even if their primary role is cognitive?

Yes.

Competitive sport is just as much mental as it is physical. In many cases, the psychological battle before competition matters just as much as the execution during it. Focus, composure, decision-making, and emotional control all shape what you’re able to do with your body.

Athletes often describe peak performance as being “in the zone.” It’s that state where everything feels automatic—your reactions are quicker, your movements are cleaner, and you’re not overthinking anything. You’re just responding.

There’s a neurological basis for that state.

When athletes are “in the zone,” brain activity tends to shift toward alpha and low-beta wave patterns—a balance between relaxed awareness and active focus. Too much high-beta activity (which is associated with stress and overthinking) can make you tense, reactive in the wrong ways, and mentally cluttered. Too little activation, and you’re sluggish or disengaged.

The sweet spot is controlled alertness: calm, but ready.

This is where different cognitive systems—attention, emotional regulation, motor coordination, and timing—start working together efficiently. When that happens, trained movements require less conscious effort. You’re not thinking through each step. You’re executing.

And because the brain controls all movement, any inefficiency at the cognitive level shows up physically. Mental fatigue slows reaction time. Stress disrupts coordination. Overthinking delays decisions.

That’s why the idea of cognitive support for athletes isn’t just theoretical—it’s practical.

The right nootropics can help support this “zone” state by influencing the systems that regulate focus, stress response, neurotransmitter balance, and brainwave activity.

Mind Lab Pro uses a variety of ingredients to achieve this. For example:

- Ingredients like L-theanine are known for promoting relaxed focus—helping reduce excessive mental noise without causing sedation.
- Compounds such as citicoline and tyrosine support neurotransmitters involved in attention, motivation, and decision-making.
- Adaptogens like rhodiola rosea can help regulate stress response, which is critical for staying composed under pressure.

The goal isn’t stimulation for the sake of stimulation. In fact, too much stimulation can push you out of the zone.

The goal is efficient brain function—a state where you’re alert, focused, and calm enough for your training to express itself without interference.

Not all nootropics improve athletic performance. But the best ones support the mental systems that allow physical performance to happen in the first place: focus, reaction time, emotional control, and the ability to stay locked in when it matters most.Energy and Stamina

Without sufficient energy, athletes simply cannot perform.

All physical and cognitive activity ultimately depends on cellular energy production, primarily ATP. When energy levels are low—whether from poor sleep, overtraining, or mental fatigue—both physical output and decision-making decline.

Many athletes turn to stimulants for a quick energy boost. While stimulants can temporarily increase alertness, they often work by forcing the nervous system into a heightened state of activation. Over time, this can increase fatigue, disrupt recovery, and in many cases violate anti-doping rules.

Effective nootropics take a different approach. Instead of forcing stimulation, they support the biological systems that produce energy in the first place. By improving mitochondrial efficiency, supporting neurotransmitter production, and increasing cerebral blood flow, certain nootropics may help sustain both mental and physical stamina during training and competition.

### Motivation and Intensity

Even highly disciplined athletes go through periods where motivation drops and training starts to feel like going through the motions.

At the neurological level, motivation is tied to dopamine and norepinephrine, but it also shows up in brainwave activity. When motivation is high, the brain tends to operate in a stable beta range—alert, engaged, and ready to act. When motivation drops, activity can shift toward lower-frequency patterns, which is where that sluggish, low-drive feeling comes from.

There’s also the opposite problem. Too much high-beta activity, which is associated with stress, can make you feel wired but unfocused. You have energy, but it’s scattered and hard to direct.

The ideal state for training is controlled activation. You want enough beta activity to drive effort and intensity, but without tipping into stress or mental overload. Some nootropics support this by improving catecholamine signaling and overall brain energy. When the brain can sustain that balanced activation, motivation becomes more consistent and less dependent on willpower alone.

Discipline matters, but it still depends on the systems that make sustained effort possible.

### Attention and Multitasking

High-level sports demand more than focus. They require the ability to process multiple streams of information at once while still executing with precision.

Athletes are constantly tracking movement, spacing, timing, and positioning. At the same time, they’re performing trained actions without consciously thinking through each step. That only works when the brain is operating in the right state.

This is where brainwave balance becomes important. Performance tends to be strongest when alpha and low-beta activity work together. Alpha helps reduce mental noise and filter out irrelevant information, while low-beta supports active focus and decision-making.

If beta activity gets too high, especially under pressure, it can lead to overthinking and hesitation. If activation is too low, processing slows down and reactions lag. The zone sits between those extremes. You’re calm enough to stay composed, but active enough to respond quickly and correctly.

Nootropics that support working memory and information processing can help maintain that balance, making it easier to stay locked in on what matters while ignoring distractions.

### Stress Management

Training and competition place real stress on both the body and the brain.

One of the clearest effects of stress is a shift toward high-beta brainwave activity. This is the state associated with anxiety, tension, and reactive behavior. It can be useful in short bursts, but when it sticks around, it interferes with timing, coordination, and decision-making.

At the same time, intense training activates the HPA axis and raises cortisol levels. Chronically elevated cortisol can impact recovery, mood, and cognitive performance. Physical exertion also increases oxidative stress, which contributes to long-term strain on the body.

To perform consistently, athletes need to be able to come down from that high-beta state and return to a more controlled level of activation. That’s where alpha and low-beta activity come back into play, allowing for composure and precision under pressure.

Some nootropics support this by helping regulate the stress response and promoting a more stable mental state. Others provide antioxidant support that helps counteract the physical demands of training.

The goal is not to feel relaxed in the sense of being slow or disengaged. It’s to stay composed, clear-headed, and responsive when the pressure is high.

All of these systems—motivation, attention, and stress regulation—come down to how efficiently the brain shifts between different states.

Performance isn’t just about effort. It’s about being in the right state at the right time, and having the control to stay there when it matters.

## **Mind Lab Pro Ingredients Overview**

Mind Lab Pro is built around a simple idea: athletic performance is not just about physical output. It is also about how well your brain can generate energy, stay focused, manage stress, and make fast decisions under pressure.

That is why the formula does not rely on one flashy ingredient. Instead, it uses a stack of compounds that target multiple areas of cognitive performance simultaneously.

At a high level, citicoline, phosphatidylserine, and the B vitamins help support brain energy, neurotransmitter activity, and overall cognitive function. These are the ingredients that help keep the mental engine running.

Lion’s Mane and Bacopa monnieri are more geared toward long-term brain support, neuroplasticity, memory, and learning. For athletes, that matters because skill development is really just learning through repetition. The better your brain is at wiring and reinforcing patterns, the better you can ingrain technique and react without hesitation.

N-Acetyl L-Tyrosine and Rhodiola rosea are especially relevant for athletes because they are tied more directly to performance under stress. They may help support catecholamine activity, mental energy, resilience, and focus when you are tired, under pressure, or pushing hard in training.

L-theanine brings a different kind of value. It is not there to hype you up. It is there to help smooth out mental noise and support a calm but alert state. For sports that require precision, timing, and composure, that matters more than people think.

Then you have maritime pine bark extract, which is included for its antioxidant and circulation-support benefits. Better blood flow and better protection against oxidative stress can support both brain performance and recovery from demanding training.

Taken together, the formula is designed to support multiple aspects of the mind-body connection simultaneously: mental energy, reaction speed, stress management, working memory, focus, and long-term brain health.

That broad-spectrum design is what makes Mind Lab Pro different from many nootropics that rely on just one mechanism, like stimulation or memory support.

I’m keeping this ingredient overview at a high level here because this article is focused specifically on Mind Lab Pro for athletes. If you want the full ingredient-by-ingredient breakdown, including the research behind each compound, [read my general Mind Lab Pro review.](/mind-lab-pro-review)

## **What the Research Says About Mind Lab Pro for Athletes**

If you’re an athlete, the most exciting thing about the Mind Lab Pro research is not memory. It’s not mood. It’s not some vague feeling of being “more dialed in.”

It’s reaction time.

That matters because reaction time is one of the hardest performance variables to improve. Strength can be built. Conditioning can be pushed. Technique can be sharpened through repetition. But the ability to process information faster, make the right decision under pressure, and time your movement precisely enough to act on that decision? That’s where performance starts getting separated for real.

And that’s exactly what the first Mind Lab Pro study measured.

This wasn’t one of those soft supplement studies where participants were asked whether they “felt sharper” or “felt more focused.”

Researchers used objective cognitive testing and measured performance in milliseconds. In a 30-day, double-blind, placebo-controlled study of 105 healthy adults, they tested three factors directly relevant to performance: simple reaction time, choice reaction time, and anticipation timing.

The results are the kind that athletes should pay attention to.

On simple reaction time, the Mind Lab Pro group improved from 330.5 milliseconds to 290 milliseconds. That’s a 40.5 millisecond improvement—about 12% faster. The placebo group actually got slightly worse over the same period, moving from 309.5 milliseconds to 318.5 milliseconds.

Twelve percent may not sound dramatic until you understand how thin the margins are in sports.

In sprinting, the difference between reacting to the starting gun in 150 milliseconds versus 190 milliseconds can mean losing the race before the first step is fully extended. In baseball, a 95-mph fastball reaches the plate in roughly 400 milliseconds.

The batter has about half that time to recognize the pitch, decide to swing, and begin the movement. If your brain processes the pitch even a fraction faster, you gain precious time to adjust your swing.

In soccer, it might mean reacting quickly enough to intercept a through pass before the striker breaks free. In tennis, it can mean recognizing the direction of a serve early enough to move into position rather than reaching for the ball late. In hockey, it might be the difference between deflecting a puck in front of the net and watching it slide past you.

Those improvements may only be measured in milliseconds, but in fast sports, those milliseconds are often the margin between success and failure.

And if an athlete already has a trained reaction time, improvements compound even more. A small percentage gain applied to an already fast nervous system can push an athlete from “very good” territory toward elite responsiveness.

But simple reaction time is still the simplified version of reality. Most sports are not “see one thing, do one thing.” Real competition involves recognizing multiple possibilities and selecting the correct action under pressure. That’s why the choice reaction time results are arguably even more important.

In the choice reaction test, participants had to identify the correct stimulus and select one of several possible responses. That’s much closer to what happens in real competition.

Here, the Mind Lab Pro group improved from 432.5 milliseconds to 388 milliseconds, a 44.5 millisecond gain—or about 10% faster decision processing. The placebo group again declined slightly, slowing from 551 milliseconds to 581 milliseconds.

This is where the athletic implications become even more interesting.

In basketball, reacting isn’t enough—you have to react correctly. A defender reading a pick-and-roll must decide instantly whether to switch, hedge, or fight over the screen. Being 40 milliseconds faster in processing that situation can be the difference between cutting off the drive and watching the ball handler glide past.

In baseball, the batter isn’t just reacting to the pitch; they’re deciding whether it’s a fastball, slider, or curve and whether it will land inside the strike zone. That recognition-and-decision window happens extremely quickly. Faster processing allows the batter to commit to the swing with better timing.

In American football, a linebacker reading the offensive line has a fraction of a second to determine whether the play is a run, pass, or misdirection. Being able to process that information even slightly faster helps them fill the correct gap before the running back breaks through.

In soccer or hockey, defenders constantly evaluate whether to close space, pass off coverage, or step into the passing lane. That’s choice reaction time in action—processing multiple possibilities and selecting the right motor response quickly enough for it to matter.

And in many of these sports, 40–45 milliseconds can absolutely matter. It may not look dramatic on paper, but in a live play, those tiny time advantages accumulate.

Then there is anticipation timing, which might be the most sport-specific measure of all.

In this test, participants had to predict when a moving light would reach a target and respond with precise timing. The Mind Lab Pro group reduced their timing error from 0.15 seconds to 0.08 seconds. That’s about a 70-millisecond improvement and nearly a 47% reduction in timing error. The placebo group again got worse.

This matters because athletic performance isn’t just about reacting quickly. It’s about being calibrated to the timing of events.

Think about a baseball batter again. Hitting a fastball isn’t just about recognizing the pitch; it’s about initiating the swing at exactly the right moment so the bat meets the ball in the hitting zone. Swing too early, and you pull off the ball. Swing too late, and the ball sails past the bat.

In tennis, returning a serve requires predicting the ball’s trajectory and arriving at the right position at the right moment. In soccer, timing a run behind the defense requires accelerating at precisely the moment a teammate releases the pass. Too early and you’re offside. Too late, and the opportunity disappears.

In basketball, rebounding often comes down to timing the jump correctly as the ball comes off the rim. In volleyball, blocking a spike requires jumping at the precise moment the hitter makes contact.

These are timing problems as much as they are strength or skill problems.

So when you see a supplement associated with roughly 12% faster simple reactions, 10% faster complex decisions, and nearly 47% improvement in anticipation timing after only 30 days, that should get your attention.

These improvements won’t turn anyone into a superhero overnight. But they represent the type of marginal gains that accumulate in competitive environments. Small increases in processing speed and timing precision can compound across hundreds of decisions in a game or match.

And this is why reaction time research is so interesting to me personally. Reaction speed and decision processing are notoriously difficult to improve. Training can help. Experience helps even more. Pattern recognition develops over time. But measurable improvements in cognitive processing speed are rare enough that when you see them in a placebo-controlled study, they deserve a closer look.

The second Mind Lab Pro study focused on memory rather than speed, but it still matters to athletes.

In a 30-day, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial involving 49 healthy adults, researchers used the Wechsler Memory Scale-IV to measure improvements in auditory memory, visual memory, immediate recall, and delayed recall.

The Mind Lab Pro group improved auditory memory by about 13%, visual memory by about 22%, immediate recall by about 11%, and delayed recall by about 16%. The placebo group improved slightly in some categories—which is normal in repeated testing, but the supplement group improved much more.

For athletes, this matters because performance isn’t just about reacting in the moment. It’s also about learning and retaining patterns.

Visual memory supports recognizing formations, positioning, and spatial patterns during play. Auditory memory helps athletes retain instructions from coaches or teammates during fast-paced situations. Immediate recall helps encode new strategies or adjustments during practice. Delayed recall ensures those lessons remain available later, when competition begins.

In other words, Study 1 showed faster processing speed, while Study 2 showed stronger memory and recall.

Speed without memory is chaotic. Memory without speed is slow. Athletes need both.

They need to see faster, decide faster, and move at the right time—but they also need to remember patterns, recognize situations, and apply what they’ve learned under pressure.

That combination is what makes the research on Mind Lab Pro particularly interesting. Instead of improving just one narrow cognitive function, the data suggest improvements across the system: reaction speed, decision processing, timing precision, and memory performance.

For athletes, that translates to the potential for sharper reactions, faster decisions, better timing, and stronger retention of the patterns that drive high-level performance.

And if you care most about reaction time—as I do—that first study is the headline. Because in sports where fractions of a second decide everything, even modest cognitive improvements can turn into real competitive advantages.

### **My Experience With Mind Lab Pro in Boxing**

Boxing is the sport I compete in. That alone should qualify it as a sport where reaction time and decision-making matter—a lot. That’s exactly why this supplement caught my attention in the first place.

I didn’t walk into sparring with a stopwatch, so I can’t give you a precise measurement of how much my reaction time improved. But I can tell you something very real: punches that used to get through and hit me… stopped landing.

That’s notable because my sparring partners are not slouches.

I regularly spar with Kiante Irving, Atif Oberlton, and Ivan Dychko. Ivan is a two-time Olympic bronze medalist, Atif is rated by the WBC, and Kiante is a national amateur champion.

I’m not wiping the floor with these guys or anything like that. They’re more experienced than me, and two of them are much faster. But on Mind Lab Pro, I can keep up.

I’m seeing punches sooner. I’m parrying shots that used to land. I’m putting together counters that keep the rounds competitive. I still get hit sometimes—that’s boxing—but it’s happening far less often.

And I know this isn’t just familiarity.

I’d already been sparring these guys for months—and in Kiante’s case, for years. I’m also 41 years old now, which means the normal expectation is that I should be slowing down. And to be honest, I was.

But with Mind Lab Pro, something changed.

This has been the most exciting effect for me personally. What I’m experiencing lines up with the reaction-time research on the formula, and the improvement is not trivial. It feels like accessing another gear of cognitive performance.

If you’re an athlete—especially in a sport where reaction time and decision-making matter—this is worth trying.

If this breakdown helped you, [grab Mind Lab Pro through any link on this page](https://www.mindlabpro.com/?a_aid=698b400c4a47f&a_bid=6d45f5c3). It doesn’t cost you anything extra, and I get a small commission for the referral.

# Mind Lab Pro Review: Does It Work or Is It Overpriced?

- **Source:** https://edlatimore.com/mind-lab-pro-review

## Body (verbatim, study/swipe)

## Is Mind Lab Pro Worth It?

***Mind Lab Pro is a legitimate, research-backed nootropic designed for long-term cognitive performance—not short-term stimulation. It works best for people who want sustained focus, improved memory, and better mental endurance, but it requires consistent use for 2–4 weeks to see full results. At $62–$69/month, it’s a premium option best suited for serious users, not casual buyers.***

As a boxer and content creator, I’m always on the lookout for anything that will give me a cognitive advantage and allow me to get more of my mental capacities. Nootropics—supplements (and sometimes drugs) that are purported to enhance focus, reaction speed, and creativity—are naturally something I look into.

There are a variety of effective options out there, many of which I’ve tried and reviewed. And, unlike most reviewers, any nootropic or supplement I take also has to be permitted for use by the World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA).

Most people looking into nootropics don’t have to consider WADA, but if that’s you, don’t worry. I wanted to start this review with that disclosure because, while most people reading this won’t have to consider it, for those who do, the impact is significant enough to warrant bringing it up.

Mind Lab Pro is a comprehensive nootropic formula that claims to enhance focus, memory, creativity, and numeracy. Given my boxing and writing careers, along with my current goal of learning European Portuguese to a B1 level and raising my chess rating to 2000, I am in a position to meaningfully evaluate whether this formula delivers on its claims.

Let’s dig into this review of Mind Lab Pro and see if it’s worth the hype.

***Throughout this article, there will be affiliate links to Mind Lab Pro. If you purchase, I get a small commission at no extra cost to you.***

## What is Mind Lab Pro

Mind Lab Pro is marketed as a universal nootropic built around 11 active ingredients designed to support overall cognitive performance.

It’s not built around a single pathway like “dopamine boost” or “stimulant drive.” Instead, it targets multiple systems involved in focus, memory, stress resilience, and mental clarity.

A few important design choices stand out:

- Stimulant-free — no caffeine, no amphetamine analogs, no DMAA-type compounds
- Two capsules per serving, taken daily
- Designed for consistent use rather than occasional spikes
- Fully disclosed ingredient amounts (no proprietary blends)

The company behind Mind Lab Pro is Performance Lab, a UK-based supplement manufacturer that positions itself around “clean label” design and evidence-based formulations.

They claim third-party testing for purity and quality control. While it’s not third-party sport-certified (like NSF Certified for Sport or Informed Sport), it presents itself as a transparency-first product compared to many competitors in the nootropic space.

In short, it’s trying to be the “clean, sustainable” option in a category full of aggressive marketing.

## What Mind Lab Pro Is Not

This is where expectations matter.

Mind Lab Pro is not:

- A stimulant stack
- Prescription medication
- A pre-workout
- An instant “Limitless” pill

It’s not Adderall or Modafinil. It’s not going to override sleep deprivation or push you into hyperfocus against your will.

It’s also not a pre-workout gym product. If you’re looking for something to blast you into a heavy lift session or a fight camp sparring day, this isn’t that.

However, it does work great for improving your ability to train if you use it consistently. Especially if your sport relies on reaction time and mental endurance.

Mind Lab Pro is subtle by design. That subtlety is either a strength or a weakness, depending on what you’re trying to accomplish.

The logic behind Mind Lab Pro is multi-pathway cognitive support. Instead of hammering one neurotransmitter system, it spreads support across several domains:

- **Neurotransmitter Support.** Ingredients like citicoline and N-Acetyl L-Tyrosine support acetylcholine and dopamine pathways — critical for focus, working memory, and motivation.
- **Stress Modulation.** Adaptogens like rhodiola aim to reduce cognitive fatigue and help regulate stress hormones, keeping you out of that high-beta, anxious state where focus fragments.
- **Neuroprotection & Plasticity.** Lion’s Mane mushroom and phosphatidylserine are included to support long-term brain health, cell membrane integrity, and potential neuroplasticity.
- **Energy Metabolism.** Rather than stimulating the nervous system, Mind Lab Pro supports mitochondrial function and cellular energy production — essentially helping brain cells do their job more efficiently.

The philosophy behind Mind Lab Pro is simple:

Don’t overstimulate the brain. Rather, support the systems that allow it to perform consistently.

That philosophy aligns well with how I approach performance in general. In boxing, in writing, in training, you don’t want a spike. You want sustainable output, day in and day out.

## The Pros of Mind Lab Pro

There are a lot of good things about Mind Lab Pro. And in the increasingly competitive nootropic supplement market, there has to be.

While I haven’t tried all of the popular brands and alternatives, I’m aware of the many competitors in the space, such as Qualia Mind and NooCube.

Below are the things that I think are exceptional about Mind Lab Pro.

### Mind Lab Pro does not use caffeine

This is common in many nootropics because caffeine is a highly researched compound that has been proven to bestow nootropic-like properties, like improved reaction time, focus, and memory.

The problem with caffeine is that, despite how common it is, it can increase cortisol and catecholamines, stress hormones that can do a number on your nervous system.

It gives many people the jitters, making it more challenging to focus. Because it’s stimulant-free, it doesn’t rely on borrowed nervous system activation.

### Mind Lab Pro has an intelligently designed ingredient blend

A lot of nootropics lean on hype, stimulants, or long ingredient lists that look impressive but don’t actually deliver meaningful doses.

Mind Lab Pro takes a different approach. Instead of overwhelming you with 25 random compounds, it uses a focused formula built around research-backed ingredients.

What makes the blend powerful is that it works across multiple pathways at once. Rather than targeting just “energy” or just “mood,” the formula is designed to support:

- Neurotransmitter production
- Stress regulation
- Brain cell integrity
- Blood flow
- Long-term neuroplasticity.

Focus, memory, and creative thinking all depend on these different but interconnected systems working well together.

### No ingredients banned by WADA

It’s not always a given that a supplement will be allowed by WADA. Nothing in Mind Lab Pro is banned from competition or training, which is a huge relief because it can really take your training to the next level.

That matters because many pre-workouts and stimulant-heavy cognitive formulas contain compounds that can either directly violate WADA standards or increase contamination risk.

### Mind Lab Pro is allergen-free

Mind Lab Pro’s formula is free of common allergens, including gluten, dairy, soy, and shellfish. It’s also non-GMO, vegan, and free of artificial colors and synthetic fillers.

### Mind Lab Pro does not use proprietary blends

An issue I have with many supplements is the use of “proprietary blends.”

Proprietary blends mean the manufacturer does not have to disclose exactly what, or how much, of something is in the blend. It’s a sneaky way for companies to hide cheap products or present the illusion that you’re getting more of something than you really are.

Mind Lab Pro doesn’t do that. All of their ingredients are clearly listed on the bottle.

### Mind Lab Pro is rigorously researched

Mind Lab Pro has two clinical studies supporting the efficacy of its formula. While the research was funded by Performance Lab—the company behind Mind Lab Pro—the studies were conducted independently by researchers at the University of Leeds in the UK, and the findings were published in peer-reviewed academic journals.

Industry-funded research should always be scrutinized. With that said, the study design and results are transparent and methodologically sound. More importantly, the outcomes are consistent with the broader body of independent research on the formula’s individual ingredients.

### Mind Lab Pro offers a money-back guarantee

Mind Lab Pro is so confident in its product that it offers a 30-day, no-questions-asked money-back guarantee. If you ever wanted to try out a nootropic and were worried it’d be a waste of money, this is a pretty good deal.

## The Cons of Mind Lab Pro

While there are many positives about Mind Lab Pro, I also have to be real with you. There are some negatives. Fortunately, none of these negatives are deal breakers (at least in my opinion), but they are still good to know.

### It takes a little while to get the full effect

If you’re looking for something you can ‘feel’ in the first 15-30 minutes, this isn’t it.

While a few ingredients may produce subtle effects within the first few days, the real strength of Mind Lab Pro comes from compounds that work cumulatively over time. Improving neuroplasticity, memory, and stress resilience take time and require consistent use.

If you’re expecting an immediate, dramatic change in focus on day one, this is not it. With that said, if you’re willing to take it consistently for several weeks, there are some pretty noticeable benefits..

### Mind Lab Pro is not cheap

One bottle of Mind Lab Pro—a 30-day supply—is $69.00. This price does not include shipping; you will have to pay shipping if your order does not meet the $100 minimum to qualify for free shipping.

There are two tiers of a subscription model. The first tier, where you get one bottle per month automatically ordered, is $62.00/month, representing a 10% savings.

The second tier of the subscription model delivers 4 bottles every 4 months. This costs $207. While that sounds expensive, it’s a 25% discount on what you would have paid if you bought 4 bottles individually.

Either way, the cost of Mind Lab Pro positions it as a premium product. To be fair, with the research and design they’ve put into their product, they absolutely deserve to price it that way.

And also, in continued fairness, they offer you a 30-day, no-questions-asked, money-back guarantee.

### Mind Lab Pro has a high cost of consistency

Because Mind Lab Pro is built around cumulative, systems-level support rather than stimulants, it’s not something you can fairly judge after three days.

That means you may need two or even three months to fully assess its impact. Practically speaking, that’s a bigger financial commitment than trying a one-time performance booster. If you quit after one bottle, you likely won’t experience what the formula is actually designed to do.

Also, taking a supplement everyday like it’s an antibiotic prescription will be tough for people. We live in a “I want it yesterday” society. While I suspect that the type of person who is interested in nootropics is more likely to not have this problem, there are probably fewer of these people than I imagine.

Hell, I even had a problem remembering to take it everyday with my daily supplement stack. That’s because, despite the name, the only time I take something everyday is when I’m in training for a fight. Since I wasn’t, adherence was a little hard, but I managed to get something out of it.

### Mind Lab Pro is not third-party sport certified

While none of the ingredients in Mind Lab Pro are banned by WADA, there’s a difference between “ingredient compliant” and “third-party sport certified.” Some elite athletes prefer supplements that carry certifications such as NSF Certified for Sport or Informed Sport, which test individual batches for banned-substance contamination.

When I reviewed Mita Nutra’s supplement blend, I was impressed by their commitment to making sure their products were NSF certified. When I talked to the founder of Mita Nutra, I also found out how expensive and time consuming the process is. Unless a product is specifically marketed to athletes, there it is economically impractical to get that certification.

However, if you compete in any sport governed by WADA, you understand that contamination risk—however small—is never zero in the supplement industry. For most people, this won’t matter. For anyone competing in a sport governed by WADA, third-party sport certification would offer additional peace of mind.

## What the Scientific Research Says About Mind Lab Pro

If I’m putting something in my body — especially as a fighter — I want human data.

Mind Lab Pro has two published, double-blind, placebo-controlled trials on the finished formula. Not ingredient theory. Not animal studies. Measured cognitive outcomes in healthy adults.

Here’s what they found.

### Study 1: Mind Lab Pro improves reaction time and processing speed

In a 30-day trial with 105 healthy adults, researchers measured cognitive speed in milliseconds using three objective tests: simple reaction time, choice reaction time, and anticipation timing.

**Simple Reaction Time (one stimulus, one response)**
Mind Lab Pro group improved from 330.5 ms to 290 ms — a 12% reduction in reaction time.

The placebo group actually got slightly slower.

Twelve percent may not sound dramatic, but in environments where reactions happen in under 200 milliseconds, that’s significant.

In boxing, trained fighters typically sit in the 170–210 ms range. A 12% improvement can move someone from average into trained territory — or from trained into elite-level responsiveness.

**Choice Reaction Time (multiple options)**
Participants improved from 432.5 ms to 388 ms — about 10% faster decision processing.

Placebo again worsened slightly.

This isn’t just reacting — it’s choosing correctly under pressure. In sport, driving, trading, or any high-speed environment, 40 milliseconds can mean the difference between positioning correctly or getting caught out.

**Anticipation Timing**
Error dropped from 0.15 seconds to 0.08 seconds — a 47% reduction in timing error.

Placebo group timing actually worsened.

That’s nearly cutting mistiming in half. In boxing, slipping too early or too late gets you hit. In real life, poor timing leads to missed brakes, dropped objects, or near collisions. This isn’t about reflexes alone — it’s calibrated precision.

After 30 days:

- ~12% faster simple reactions
- ~10% faster complex decisions
- ~47% better timing accuracy

These are measurable improvements in neural throughput and motor precision.

### Study 2: Mind Lab Pro improves memory & recall

The second study measured memory using the Wechsler Memory Scale IV in 49 healthy adults over 30 days.

- **Auditory memory** improved by 13% (vs ~4% placebo).

That means better retention of spoken information — remembering instructions, names, conversations, and key details after hearing them once.

- **Visual memory** improved by 22% (vs ~1.5% placebo).

That’s stronger recall of layouts, patterns, diagrams, spatial relationships — the kind of memory used constantly in sport, business, and daily life.

- **Immediate recall** improved by 11% (vs ~3% placebo).

Better mental capture speed. You retain information the first time you hear it instead of needing repetition.

- **Delayed recall** improved by 16% (vs ~4–5% placebo).

This is long-term retention. Information doesn’t just register — it sticks.

### What This Means

Study 1 showed faster processing and sharper timing.
Study 2 showed stronger encoding and recall.

Speed without memory isn’t useful. Memory without speed isn’t efficient.

Mind Lab Pro improved both.

These aren’t superhero gains. But they are consistent, measurable, double-digit improvements in healthy adults over 30 days.

And in performance — whether in sport, business, or daily life — marginal improvements compound. You can read more about these [scientific research that goes in to Mind Lab Pro on their site here](https://www.mindlabpro.com/pages/studies/?a_aid=698b400c4a47f&a_bid=6d45f5c3).

## My Experience With Mind Lab Pro

I have to start off by saying that I was at least partially familiar with the ingredients in Mind Lab Pro because of my experience with other nootropics.

So I expected an immediate feeling of the nootropics hitting. I initially felt disappointed, but that’s only because I was not yet aware of how Mind Lab Pro works.

Believe it or not, I started jotting down my experiences for this review *before* I knew how it worked.

### Language Learning: Breaking a Plateau

I spend a lot of time working with Practice Portuguese to improve my spoken and written European Portuguese. One exercise I do consistently is a dictation drill: I listen to a clip and try to write down as much of it as possible before replaying it.

If you’re learning a language, this is one of the best exercises you can do.

I’d been doing this for months and felt stuck. I could hear the words, but I couldn’t process and capture full chunks quickly enough. It felt like I hit a ceiling.

By week two on Mind Lab Pro, something changed.

I was capturing larger chunks of sentences before needing to replay the audio. It felt like the bottleneck loosened.

### Sparring at 41: Where I Felt It Most

This is where things got real.

I’m 41. While I took nearly a decade off from boxing — which likely spared me cumulative damage — that doesn’t mean aging stopped. My reaction time had absolutely declined.

I regularly spar with younger, faster fighters. Fighters who used to get the better of me on specific combinations.

That stopped happening as often.

I started seeing shots earlier and responding cleaner. My ability to read opponents’ movement and reaction time is noticeably faster. As my coach exclaimed in surprise, “You’re much better!” For a fighter, milliseconds matter.

This has been, by far, the most exciting aspect for me. What I’m experiencing aligns with the reaction-time data on the formula, and it is not a trivial improvement. This is a whole new level of ability that can be potentially unlocked.

I’ll probably write an entire separate article on this alone.

[***Grab a bottle of Mind Lab Pro here.***](https://www.mindlabpro.com/?a_aid=698b400c4a47f&a_bid=6d45f5c3)

### Writing & SEO Work: More Industrious

Boxing is what I love, but writing is how I make my living.

Mind Lab Pro didn’t suddenly make me a more creative writer. My thinking style didn’t transform overnight.

What it did was make me more industrious.

I feel less distracted. I was less tempted to check tabs or drift into unnecessary rabbit holes. It’s easier to stay seated and execute.

More importantly, it’s easier to hold onto ideas that surface while I’m mid-paragraph. There are few things more frustrating than losing your mental place because you tried to mentally juggle a new insight.

That happens less now.

It feels like improved task persistence and better working memory.

### Chess: Better Pattern Recognition

I play a lot of chess. While there are millions of possible board combinations, strong players understand that certain position types repeatedly occur. Recognizing the structure and knowing the correct plan is what separates intermediate players from stronger ones.

I’m still making mistakes.

But my ability to recognize what type of position I’m in — and respond accordingly — has improved. I see it reflected in my accuracy rating and in the fact that I’m losing fewer games.

Maybe this is what finally gets me to 1900 on Chess.com.

Time will tell.

### Side Effects & Integration

I haven’t experienced any side effects.

No headaches. No jitteriness. No sleep disruption.

And in a supplement routine that’s already pill-heavy, adding two more capsules is negligible.

If I had to summarize my experience:

Mind Lab Pro didn’t feel like flipping on a light switch. It felt like clearing the fog.

## Mind Lab Pro vs Alternatives

Here are a list of other nootropics I have personal experience with to compare to Mind Lab Pro.

## Mind Lab Pro vs Thesis

At a high level, Mind Lab Pro and Thesis are both designed to improve focus, memory, and cognitive performance. But they go about it in completely different ways, and that difference matters more than anything else when deciding which one to use.

Mind Lab Pro is a fixed, all-in-one formula. You take the same two capsules every day, and over time, it works to improve your baseline cognitive performance across multiple systems—memory, focus, stress resilience, and mental endurance.

Thesis is a customizable system. Instead of one formula, you get multiple blends—Focus, Energy, Clarity, Motivation, Logic, Creativity—and you rotate them depending on what you’re trying to accomplish. It’s less about building a baseline and more about adjusting your mental state on demand.

### Consistency vs Customization

This is the biggest difference.

Mind Lab Pro is built for consistency. You don’t have to think about it. You take it every day, and over time, your cognitive performance improves in a steady, predictable way. It’s simple, repeatable, and designed for long-term use.

Thesis is built for customization. You experiment with different blends, figure out which ones work best for you, and use them based on the situation. That gives you more control, but it also requires more effort and trial-and-error.

If you want something that just works in the background, Mind Lab Pro fits better.

If you enjoy dialing things in and optimizing for specific tasks, Thesis gives you that flexibility.

### Intensity vs Stability

Thesis is more noticeable.

Depending on the blend, you can feel a shift relatively quickly—more focus, more drive, more engagement with whatever you’re working on. Some stacks feel closer to a mild stimulant effect, even when they’re not heavily caffeine-based.

Mind Lab Pro is more stable.

It doesn’t hit you with a noticeable “on switch.” Instead, it gradually improves how your brain performs over time—less mental fatigue, better sustained focus, smoother recall. It’s not about intensity. It’s about reducing friction.

If you want something you can feel working early on, Thesis is more likely to deliver that.

If you’re thinking in terms of compounding gains over weeks and months, Mind Lab Pro fits that model better.

### Stimulants and Energy

Mind Lab Pro is completely stimulant-free.

No caffeine, no spikes, no crashes. That makes it easier to use daily without worrying about sleep disruption or nervous system burnout.

Thesis depends on the blend.

Some formulas are stimulant-free, while others are more energizing and can feel more “activated.” That can be useful, but it also introduces more variability in how you respond day to day.

If you’re sensitive to stimulants or trying to avoid them entirely, Mind Lab Pro is the safer option.

If you’re okay with some variability in exchange for stronger acute effects, Thesis can feel more powerful in the moment.

### Ease of Use

Mind Lab Pro is simple.

Two capsules per day. No decisions. No adjustments. It integrates into your routine without friction.

Thesis requires more involvement.

You have to test different blends, pay attention to how you respond, and decide which one to use based on the task. That’s not necessarily a downside—but it does require more effort.

Mind Lab Pro is plug-and-play.

Thesis is a system you have to actively manage.

Price and Value

Both are premium products, but they justify their cost differently.

Mind Lab Pro is priced around $62–$69 per month depending on whether you subscribe. You’re paying for a consistent, research-backed formula that you can run indefinitely.

Thesis is typically more expensive on a monthly basis, depending on how you structure your subscription and how many blends you’re using. You’re paying for flexibility and customization.

Mind Lab Pro is about long-term value.

Thesis is about optionality.

### Which One Should You Choose

If your goal is to improve your baseline cognitive performance—better focus, better memory, better mental endurance over time—Mind Lab Pro is the better fit.

If your goal is to actively manipulate your mental state depending on the situation—deep work, creative work, high-output sessions—Thesis gives you more control.

They’re not really competing products.

Mind Lab Pro makes your default state better.

Thesis lets you shift your state depending on what you need.

## Mind Lab Pro vs Alpha Brain

At a high level, Alpha Brain and Mind Lab Pro aim to solve the same problem: improve focus, memory, and cognitive performance without relying on caffeine. But they approach it differently.

### Transparency vs. Proprietary Blends

The biggest structural difference is formulation transparency.

Alpha Brain uses proprietary blends. You can see the ingredients, but not the exact amounts of each compound within those blends. That doesn’t mean the formula is weak — clearly, it’s effective for many people — but it does make it harder to evaluate dose adequacy.

Mind Lab Pro, on the other hand, lists the exact dosage of each ingredient. From a biohacker or athlete perspective, that matters. If you care about knowing precisely what you’re putting into your body, Mind Lab Pro wins on transparency.

### Intensity vs. Stability

Subjectively, Alpha Brain feels more noticeable.

When it “kicks in,” there’s a distinct shift — sharper focus, easier access to flow state, and a kind of productive urgency. I’ve described it as motivation in a bottle. It’s easier to sit down and immediately engage.

Mind Lab Pro feels different. It’s less of a switch and more of a gradual elevation of baseline cognition. The improvements are steadier and more subtle — longer sustained focus, smoother recall, less mental fatigue over time — but not necessarily that immediate jolt into flow.

If you’re looking for something you can feel clearly within the first hour, Alpha Brain is more likely to deliver that sensation. If you prefer cumulative, systems-level support that builds over weeks, Mind Lab Pro fits that profile better.

## Mind Lab Pro vs. Morning Would

I’ve used both Morning Would and Mind Lab Pro, but they’re not doing the exact same thing. They overlap in the “nootropic” category, but their personalities are very different.

Morning Would is closer to a hybrid between a nootropic and a pre-workout. It’s built for output — mental and physical — especially when you’re under stress or sleep deprived. Mind Lab Pro is more of a long-term cognitive support formula. It’s designed to elevate baseline brain function rather than push performance in the moment.

### Energy vs. Baseline Cognition

Morning Would shines when you’re running on fumes.

During my son’s sleep regression, I needed something that could support decision-making, verbal clarity, training intensity, and content production all at once. Morning Would felt like a system-wide ignition switch. It didn’t just wake me up — it made me capable.

Mind Lab Pro feels different. It does not wake you up or provide a surge of energy. I would say that Mind Lab Pro is about making your best version better, while Morning Would helps you become a great version of yourself when you’re dragging from 4 hours of sleep and still need to function at a high level.

### Powder vs. Capsules

There’s also a practical difference.

Morning Would is a powdered drink. That makes it feel like a morning ritual — something you mix, sip, and associate with getting started. It contains electrolytes and has a mild salty taste, which reinforces that “performance” identity.

Mind Lab Pro is two capsules. It’s simple and clean. No ritual. No taste. Just swallow and move on.

That might not sound important, but psychologically, it changes how the supplement integrates into your day.

### Hormonal Timing and Cortisol

Morning Would pairs well with natural morning cortisol rhythms. I intentionally delayed coffee and used it during that early window to avoid stacking caffeine spikes on top of the natural cortisol surge.

Mind Lab Pro doesn’t interact with that dynamic the same way. Because it’s stimulant-free and not built around acute energy, it doesn’t feel tied to that early-morning hormonal window. It’s more neutral in that respect.

[***Read my review of Morning Would here.***](/review-morning-would-nootropic)

## Gorilla Mind Smooth vs. Mind Lab Pro

I’ve used Gorilla Mind Smooth for a full year and Mind Lab Pro for extended stretches as well. Both are stimulant-free, both support focus and cognitive performance, and both are clean enough for someone who pays attention to WADA compliance.

But they are not the same experience.

### Mood-Forward vs. Systems-Forward

Gorilla Mind Smooth (GMS) is mood-forward.

The most noticeable effect for me isn’t raw focus. It’s optimism, patience, and an elevated baseline mood. That psychological shift alone improves productivity. When you feel good, you’re more willing to engage deeply with work. GMS seems to lean heavily into dopamine and serotonin support through ingredients like L-Tyrosine, saffron, and kanna.

Mind Lab Pro feels more systems-forward.

It doesn’t noticeably boost my mood in the same way. It just makes me think more clearly, with better recall, less mental friction, and longer sustained focus blocks over time. If you need your mood elevated to unlock productivity, GMS may feel more noticeable.

If your mood is already stable and you’re looking to refine cognitive performance, Mind Lab Pro feels more foundational.

### Athletic Performance Angle

From a training standpoint, Gorilla Mind Smooth feels slightly more performance-oriented.

The 600mg Alpha GPC dose is substantial and supported by research on power output and reaction time. I’ve noticed improved sharpness during sparring sessions and long workouts. It doesn’t feel like a pre-workout, but it’s performance-adjacent.

Mind Lab Pro is less about explosiveness and more about mental endurance. I wouldn’t necessarily reach for it before a hard sparring session for a reaction-time edge. I would take it during periods where I want consistent mental clarity across training, writing, studying, and recovery.

GMS is slightly more tactical in athletic settings.

Mind Lab Pro is more strategic.

### Subtlety vs. Accumulation

Both are stimulant-free, but GMS is easier to “feel” early on — mostly because of the mood lift.

Mind Lab Pro is quieter and takes weeks to build. If you’re expecting something dramatic in the first 30 minutes, GMS is more likely to deliver that sensation.

If you’re thinking in terms of compounding gains over months — the same way you’d think about strength training — Mind Lab Pro fits that mindset better.

[***Read my review of Gorilla Mind Smooth here.***](/gorilla-mind-smooth-review)

## Mind Lab Pro vs. White Maeng Da Kratom

I’m going to be very direct here: **White Maeng Da Kratom and Mind Lab Pro aren’t really competing products**, even though people use both for focus. I just happen to have tried White Maeng Da Kratom out, so it’s something I have direct experience with.

Mind Lab Pro is a supplement designed for long-term cognitive support. White Maeng Da is a psychoactive plant with real performance effects and real risk. One is a daily foundation. The other is a tool you pull out when you need a result.

### Immediate Punch vs. Slow Build

White Maeng Da is fast.

When it hits, it’s obvious: calm alertness, less mental noise, and a strong “locked in” feeling that makes distraction harder. For me, the effect showed up within about 20–30 minutes and made it easier to stay on one task without getting pulled into mental clutter.

Mind Lab Pro doesn’t work like that. It’s not a “feel it in 30 minutes” product. If you want something you can feel today, kratom wins.

If you want something that builds a better baseline over time, Mind Lab Pro wins.

### Risk Profile: Tool vs. Lifestyle

This is the biggest difference.

Mind Lab Pro is a pretty low risk supplement. It’s stimulant-free, built from commonly used nootropic ingredients, and meant to be taken consistently.

White Maeng Da interacts with opioid receptors and adrenergic receptors. That’s why it can feel so effective—but it’s also why it can become a problem if you treat it like a daily supplement.

Tolerance builds, dependency is possible, and the unregulated vendor landscape adds another layer of risk.

Mind Lab Pro is a “use it daily” product.

White Maeng Da is “use it sparingly and deliberately.”

### Focus Quality: Calm Drive vs. Clean Cognition

Kratom gives what I’d call “calm drive.”

It doesn’t just make you alert like caffeine. It reduces the background stress response and pulls you into a steadier mental state, making it easier to stay on-task. That’s especially useful when you’re sleep-deprived and still have to produce.

Mind Lab Pro gives clean cognition.

It’s less about drive and more about making your brain feel like it’s running with less friction—better sustained focus, smoother memory access, and less afternoon cognitive drag.

Kratom is better when the problem is “my brain is too loud.”

Mind Lab Pro is better when the problem is “my brain is getting tired.”

### WADA and Competitive Use

From a competition perspective, Mind Lab Pro is simpler.

It’s easier to evaluate, easier to standardize, and has a lower contamination risk profile than buying kratom from the open market. Even if kratom is legal where you live, that doesn’t automatically make it a smart choice for an athlete governed by strict anti-doping rules—especially because the supplement market is notorious for contamination and inconsistent labeling.

Honestly, anyone who uses kratom while competing is already playing a crazy game.

If you’re an athlete Mind Lab Pro is the safer lane.

[***Read my review of White Maeng Da Kratom here.***](/white-maeng-da-kratom-for-focus)

## Frequently asked questions about MindLab Pro

How long does Mind Lab Pro take to work?

Mind Lab Pro does not produce immediate effects like caffeine or stimulants. Some users notice subtle improvements within a few days, but most benefits—such as improved memory, focus, and reaction time—become noticeable after 2–4 weeks of consistent use. This is because many of its ingredients support long-term brain function and neuroplasticity rather than short-term stimulation.

  Is Mind Lab Pro legit or a scam?

Mind Lab Pro is a legitimate nootropic supplement produced by Performance Lab. It uses fully disclosed ingredients, has published human clinical trials on the finished formula, and offers a 30-day money-back guarantee. While it is not FDA-approved (like all supplements), there is no credible evidence suggesting it is a scam.

  Does Mind Lab Pro actually improve focus and memory?

Research on Mind Lab Pro’s formula shows measurable improvements in reaction time, memory, and processing speed in healthy adults. Users often report better sustained focus, improved working memory, and reduced mental fatigue, especially after consistent use over several weeks.

  What are the side effects of Mind Lab Pro?

Most users report no significant side effects. Because it is stimulant-free, it does not typically cause jitteriness, crashes, or sleep disruption. However, individual reactions may vary depending on sensitivity to ingredients like bacopa or tyrosine.

  Is Mind Lab Pro worth the price?

Mind Lab Pro costs between $62–$69 per month, making it a premium nootropic. It may be worth the price for individuals who rely heavily on cognitive performance—such as knowledge workers, students, and athletes—but may not be ideal for those looking for a quick or inexpensive boost.

## Final Verdict On MindLab Pro

Mind Lab Pro is a premium, stimulant-free nootropic designed for steady cognitive performance rather than dramatic short-term stimulation. It’s built for consistency, not intensity.

If you understand that going in, you’ll evaluate it correctly.

**Strengths:**

- Caffeine-free and smooth
- Fully disclosed ingredient amounts (no proprietary blends)

**Weaknesses:**

**Ideal User:**

- Students preparing for long study cycles
- Athletes looking for WADA-compliant cognitive support
- People who want steady clarity without caffeine spikes
- Individuals sensitive to stimulants

**Not Ideal User:**

- Anyone expecting a “Limitless pill” effect
- Users looking for a noticeable jolt of energy
- People looking for intense pre-workout-style stimulation
- Budget buyers who prefer DIY stacking
- Those unwilling to commit to 2–4 weeks of consistent use

\*\*Overall Rating:
\*\*8.5 / 10

It scores high for formulation quality, transparency, and long-term sustainability. It loses points for price and lack of third-party sport certification.

# Man Greens Review (90 Days + Bloodwork): Does It Actually Boost Testosterone?

## Body (verbatim, study/swipe)

Does Man Greens Actually Boost Testosterone? (Quick Answer)

Yes—based on both ingredient science and my personal results, Man Greens can support natural testosterone production.

After 90 days, my testosterone measured at 740 ng/dL at age 40, which is well above the average range for my age group.

That said, results depend on your baseline, lifestyle, and consistency.

***If you’re searching for a Man Greens review, you’ve seen claims about boosting testosterone, energy, and recovery. In this in-depth review, I test the product over 90 days, show my lab results, compare ingredient science, and reveal what really works—and what doesn’t***

I’m 40 years old, and nearly 8 years have passed since [my last professional fight](/why-i-quit-boxing). After that bout ended in a draw, I spent the next few years going back and forth on whether I’d ever step into the ring again. But [after training](/boxing-training) with Roy Jones Jr. to help him prepare for his fight against Mike Tyson, I finally made up my mind: I would never fight professionally again.

Or so I thought.

After I got my book deal, I dove into the strategies behind successful books like David Goggins’ *Can’t Hurt Me*. One thing became clear: the best way to [make my book a](https://amzn.to/41wKkJD) success [was to fight again.](/boxing-at-40)

It’s exciting—but at 40 years old, I need to be in the best shape possible. I need to be better than optimal, because now I’m battling time as well as another man. And most of those men will be younger, stronger, and in their athletic primes. On top of that, because I’m competing in a sport governed by the World Anti-Doping Agency, I can’t take any of the usual anti-aging treatments like TRT, anabolic steroids, or peptide hormones.

Confronting the reality of my age, I knew I needed something to keep me in the game—not just to get an edge. I went looking for a way to naturally raise my testosterone, improve my recovery, and make my body the best version it could be at 40.

That’s when I found [Mita Nutra’s](https://mitanutra.com/products/man-greens?dt_id=2582763&selling_plan=6036062430) [Man Greens.](https://mitanutra.com/products/man-greens?dt_id=2582763&selling_plan=6036062430)
